This PR migrates our authentication middleware from express-session to a custom JWT-based solution. We move from server-side sessions to stateless tokens to scale across multiple regions, fix the cookie-domain bug that was breaking single sign-on across subdomains, and remove the Redis session-store dependency. New refresh-token rotation logic with a sliding 7-day window. Existing endpoints are unchanged.
Postmortem Polish: Outage 2026-04-18: API war 47 Minuten down, von 14:03 bis 14:50. Marcus hat gegen 13:55 ein Deployment angestoßen, das eine alte Version der Auth-Library mitgebracht hat — niemand hat den Lockfile-Diff im Review angesehen. 14:03 fingen die ersten 500er an. Niemand hat reagiert weil der Slack-Channel auf "muted" stand bei Anna, die On-Call hatte. Marcus hat den Outage erst um 14:30 gesehen als Kunden sich bei Sales gemeldet haben. Rollback dauerte weitere 20 Min weil unser Rollback-Script kaputt war, hat ihn niemand mehr getestet seit Q1. Wir müssen den Lockfile-Review wieder verpflichtend machen, das Slack-Mute-Verhalten klären, und das Rollback-Script in CI testen.

Incident 2026-04-22: search latency P99 climbed from 80ms to 4500ms over a 6-hour window starting around 02:00 UTC. The on-call engineer didn't notice because the alert threshold was set too high (P99 < 5s) — by the time it fired, customers in EMEA had already filed 11 tickets. Root cause: a gradual fragmentation of our Elasticsearch index due to a missed daily reindex job that has been silently failing for the past 3 weeks. Contributing: our cron alert pipeline was changed two months ago and the failure notifications were going to a deprecated Slack channel. Remediation: tightened the latency alert to P99 < 500ms, fixed the reindex job, audited all cron alerts for deprecated routes, and added a synthetic check that reads from search every 5 minutes.

The product team identified three blockers in this sprint that need to be addressed before we can ship the new pricing tier: the billing service still uses the old Stripe SDK that doesn't support metered billing, the admin panel doesn't have a way to grant trial extensions, and there's no notification system for usage limits being approached. The team estimates two weeks of work to clear these blockers, assuming no further surprises.

TL;DR: In den letzten sechs Monaten haben wir unser Onboarding komplett überarbeitet. Statt eines 45-minütigen Live-Demos mit Sales bekommen Neukunden jetzt einen interaktiven Produkt-Walkthrough, der sich an ihre Rolle anpasst (Admin, Developer, End-User). Die Conversion vom Trial zum Paid stieg von 14% auf 23%, die Time-to-Value sank von durchschnittlich 12 auf 4 Tage, und der Support reportet 30% weniger Onboarding-bezogene Tickets. Die Investition (drei Engineers, zwei Designer, vier Monate) hat sich nach acht Wochen amortisiert.

Last quarter we ran an experiment to remove our pricing page entirely and instead route every visitor to a "talk to sales" form. Hypothesis: high-intent buyers convert better when forced into conversation, and we'd compensate the volume drop with deal size. Result: total revenue dropped 22% even though average contract value rose 35% — the math doesn't work because we lost too many self-serve customers who would never have talked to sales but ended up buying anyway. We restored the pricing page and learned that "channel forcing" is a real risk in PLG models.
